Name: gtif-site-itentification-wind
Title: Site Identification Wind
Description:
For the identification of potential wind energy areas, a number of parameters and criteria can be selected in the left-hand box:

**Terrain: elevation and terrain slope:**

Strongly inclined and perturbed flow conditions are unsuitable for the operation of wind turbines.

Alpine sites at high elevation might also be unsuitable for wind energy use.

Used data: 10m DEM data.

**Standoff distance to settlements:**

The minimum distance to settlements can be selected in a range from 400m to 1200m.

Used data: Settlement data provided by Energiewerkstatt.

**Standoff distance to roads, railways and overhead power lines:**

In order to hazards for public road traffic, standoff distances from wind turbines to major public roads can be specified.

As the potential hazard to road traffic very much depends on the traffic density and the driving speed, minor roads (residential roads, service roads, tracks, paths, etc.) are not taken into account.

Used data: OpenStreetMap

**Nature preservation areas:**

Nature preservation areas can be selected as exclusion criteria:

IUCN I to IV are national parks and nature preservation areas.

IUCN V and VI are protected landscapes and areas.

Used data: UBA

**Airports:**

Select Airports (with 4 km buffer) and Airfields (with 2 km buffer) as exclusion criteria.

Used data: OpenStreetMap and Austro Control

**Water bodies:**

Select water bodies, including glaciated areas, as exclusion criteria.

Used data: OpenStreetMap

**Military areas:**

Select military training areas as exclusion criteria.

Used data: OpenStreetMap

For the calculation of wind energy potentials, the area of interest can be specified by opening a rectangle in the map view. The parameters for this calculation can be selected in the right-hand box.

Within the specified box, hypothetical wind turbines with the specified characteristics (hub height, rotor diameter, rated power) are positioned by an algorithm seeking the optimum locations within the available area. The minimum distance between the individual turbines’ positions can be specified between three and five rotor diameters.

For each wind turbine, the annual energy production is then calculated based on the local wind power density and assuming typical production characteristics for a wind turbine of the current generation.

The wind energy potential in the considered domain is then expressed as a number of wind turbines that fit into that area and as the total annual energy, that these turbines might produce at these locations.The wind data presented on this page is based on long-term meteorological simulations, that have been interpolated and adjusted according to terrain, ground cover and in-situ wind measurements. The accuracy of the results very much depends on the local terrain complexity and the coverage of wind data. In addition, local wind systems, e.g. katabatic winds, are not covered by the numerical simulation. The data should thus be interpreted as a first indication only. In particular in complex terrain or at alpine sites, an on-site wind measurement with an adequate measurement height and duration is required to assess the wind energy potential.

No liability is assumed for the accuracy of the data.
